verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/38/48/51) is a type I integral membrane protein with its steady-state home at the trans-Golgi network, cycling between TGN and early endosomes via the cell surface. The itinerary includes a documented plasma membrane transit step — TGN46 is delivered to the PM and rapidly internalized through endosomes back to the TGN, and it has been used as a classical marker of TGN-to-PM-to-endosome cycling. Its own type I TM anchor places the ectodomain on the outer leaflet during the PM stage, though the surface pool is a minority compared to the TGN pool. Not cell-state-induced, not germline-restricted, not lysosomal exocytosis, not partner-tethered — fits dual_localization (constitutive cycling with minority surface pool).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface pool is small and transient; whether the PM residence is long enough for practical binder access is uncertain.
